Petition To Remove Trustee, For Accounting, And For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty
Off Calendar Jan-10-2023 Continued To Mar-21-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Milla L. Lvovich, Esq. for Mikhail Vaynberg (Petitioner. George Wailes, Esq. for Alexander VAynberg (Petitioner/Respondent). Andrew Schultz, Esq. for Minna Fernan (Temporary Successor Trustee of Trust B). Jeffrey Loew, Esq. and Deborah Freeland, Esq. (Litigation Counsel).

The Court addressed the two petitions. Ms. Lvovich stated that she is unable to confer with her client, that she has a duty but is unable to submit a response, that her client has diminished capacity, and that he has a doctor in Jerusalem. Ms. Lvovich also stated that when she represented her client here that he had capacity, that there was a power of attorney appointed in Israel, and that the power of attorney has been operative at all times. The Court addressed Ms. Lvovich stating that it sounds as if her client may not be able to maintain his petition. Mr. Loew stated that he wants a guardian ad litem (GAL) appointed for Mikhail Vaynberg. The Court stated that the petition before it today is brought by Mikhail Vaynberg as the trustee, that it is the trustee or no one, and inquired if Mikhail's petition should go off calendar. Brief argument is held. Mr. Wailes stated that Yafim Ratinski is named as co-trustee, that he should be removed and ordered to account, that Ms. Lvovich agreed to accept service for him, and that he filed a request for default that was rejected. The Court noted that probate does not proceed by way of default. Ms. Lvovich stated that Mr. Ratinski is not listed as a party and is not on the register of actions, that she does not represent Mr. Ratinski, and that he is the agent under the durable power of attorney. The Court noted that the prayer is pretty clear. Discussions re: the case caption are held. The Court stated that counsel may wish to perfect things, that in 3 1/2 years that its never had a dispute about who is a party, that Mikhail is a petitioner, and that if he lacks capacity that he needs a GAL. Ms. Lvovich stated that her client has a power of attorney and that the agent could be appointed as the GAL. The Court ordered the parties to meet and confer regarding a GAL for Mikhail Vaynberg. Mr. Wailes stated that he and Ms. Lvovich need to agree on the parties, that Mr. Ratinski needs to be served, and noted that Ms. Lvovich twice accepted service for Mr. Ratinski. The Court stated that the GAL could be a neutral that does not have an interest, that the parties should plan to move the matter to a resolution, and that they should meet and confer on alternative dispute resolution (ADR). The Court stated that it will give time to amend and serve. Mr. Schultz stated that administration of the trust is at a standstill, that there is a dispute about the Lombard property, that the other property was sold and there is no other U.S. property, that Trust B owns part of the Lombard rents, that no one wants to do anything until the issues are resolved, and that they hope that this would get to mediation for a global settlement. The Court stated that not all cases are purely money cases, that this appears to be a money case, that it appears to be about what belongs in each pot, and that this Court will not resolve emotional issues. The Court then continued Alexander Vaynberg's Petition To Remove Trustee, For Accounting, And For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty to March 21, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court ordered Mikhail Vaynberg's Petition For Order Determining Interest In Trust Property, Directing Trustees To Transfer Possession Of Property, Accounting, Damages, And Attorney's Fees And Costs, etc. off calendar. Mr. Wailes asked the Court if they can't agree on an amended petition to name the parties if it would consider hearing that issue on 3/21. The Court stated that it would not hear that issue on 3/21 and that it expects the parties to meet and confer in good faith on a second amended petition.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich appeared by zoom for Mikhail Vaynberg.
George Wailes appeared by zoom for Alexander Vaynberg.
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with Minna Fernan (successor trustee of trust B) by zoom.

The court asked Mr. Schultz for overall status. Mr. Schultz stated that his client has been working on the appraisals of the properties, that the appraiser has been able to view and inspect the properties although the Lombard property has tenants in it who were resistant to allowing him to do so, and that Ms. Fernan has marshalled the commercial property that had been leased, sub-leased and then apparently abandoned so she worked with the tenant council to marshal and remove the belongings from it. Mr. Schultz also stated that a sub-leaser in the commercial property installed an illegal bathroom/shower which now needs to be removed in order to rent the property, and that he thinks they are making as much progress as they possibly can at their end at this point, but they need the income and expenses from the properties for the last few years in order to complete the appraisals as well as the accountings from both parties in order to move forward. In addition, Mr. Schultz stated that Ms. Lvovich is trying to cooperate, but has had to subpoena records and that there has still been some resistance from Alex on providing information. Ms. Lvovich stated that they have been without records, that they had a second round of subpoenas, that they received thousands of pages of records in November, that she believes their data is as good as it's ever going to get and that they have requested records back to 2006, but banks do not keep records that far back. Ms. Lvovich also stated that she has not had a chance to do the accounting, but they have the data in the form of a spread sheet, that they provided a list of the last rental amounts for the commercial property prior to abandonment, that they don't have any other information, that costs and receipts were intermingled, and that she has seen some records going back to 2010/2011, but she is not sure if there are any records going further back. Mr. Wailes stated that Alex's accounting is essentially complete, that there are a couple of issues they are working on in terms of disclosure, that there are two properties Alex assisted in repairing, that Alex only had rental income from the residential part of the Lombard property, and that they are trying to put that in the accounting in a sensible way. Mr. Wailes also stated that Alex is owed $500,000.00 from the trust, that they haven't included Panorama into the $500,000.00 and that he estimates he can turn over the data files in two weeks. Discussion was held about the parties having to sit down and make sense of the numbers, the need to do some discovery, the issue of the nephew having been appointed as co-trustee and the Panorama tax issue. All parties indicated that they understand that Minna Fernan is the only trustee of trust B. The parties are exchange the accountings amongst themselves by January 28th. A joint status report is to be filed by February 15th. The Petition for Order Determining Interest in Trust Property, etc., Petition to Remove Trustee, and status of the accountings from Alex and Mikhail are continued to February 22, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Milla Lvovich appeared by zoom for Mikhail Vaynberg (Trustor/Beneficiary).
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with Minna Fernan (Successor Trustee) by zoom.
George Whales appeared by zoom for Alexander Vaynberg (Respondent/Petitioner).

At the first calling of the matter, Mr. Whales was not present. The matter was passed and recalled at the end of the calendar to allow Mr. Whales the opportunity to make an appearance. Ms. Lvovich stated that they have not discussed a March date regarding transitioning of the property, that she is putting the accounting together, and that she has sent out subpoenas for bank records, but one institution responded that they have no records and the other responded that they needed more time to respond. Ms. Lvovich also stated that her client did not keep records and that a provision in the trust states that no accountings are required during his lifetime. Mr. Whales stated that he thought they agreed on a date of March 1, 2016 and that Mikhail needs to account as co-owner. The court stated that the accountings will be presented according to the court order. Ms. Lvovich stated that she does not remember agreeing to a date, that she is in the dark and that she has no records. Ms. Lvovich also stated that Lombard Street is owned 45% by trust B and 65% by Alexander, that the Panorama property is included in trust B and was allocated 100% to the trust when the wife died, that it turns out that in 1989 Alexander was part owner and that her client is willing to provide 30% of this property. The court stated that it will not reconsider the scope of the order and makes it clear to Mr. Whales that it is not his best interest to request more information. Ms. Schultz stated that he believes there is cooperation and that the appraisals are moving forward on both of the trust's properties. Mr. Whales stated that Alexander's accounting is very close. The Petition to Remove Trustee, etc., Petition for Order Determining Interest in Trust Property, etc., and status of the accountings (2) are continued to January 4, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Court noted that a stipulation has been submitted as well as a declaration of consent from Bank of America for the appointment of Ms. Fernan, stated that there is concern as the stipulation goes beyond what the Court does by way of a stipulation, referred to the language in paragraph 6 regarding the sale proceeds of the property and stated that when it looks at the trust, it does not see anything regarding the property. Mr. Wailes stated that there is no dispute by any party that Trust B owned two thirds of the property since the trust was created in 2006, that he does not believe there is any concern for the Court to strike that, that he was going to ask the Court to strike number 5 and that he believes the Trustee can handle the funds of the trust as she sees. Mr. Schultz stated that both Counsel have expressed strong willingness to work with Ms. Fernan. The Court will issue an order suspending the current Trustee and appoint Ms. Fernan as the Temporary Trustee of Trust B.
